articles.title: NVIDIA Launches "Ising": The World's First Open AI Models for Quantum Computing

articles.title: Nobody knows how many CVEs Anthropic's Project Glasswing has actually found
articles.snippet: Like the majority of the companies participating, it remains a mystery Last week, Anthropic surprised the world by declaring that its latest model, Mythos, is so good at finding vulns that it would create chaos if released. Now, under the title of Project Glasswing, over 50 selected companies and orgs are allowed to test the hyped up LLM to find security holes in their own products. But just how many problems have they really discovered?…
articles.ai_summary: Anthropic's Project Glasswing allows select companies to test its advanced model, Mythos, for finding security vulnerabilities. However, the actual number of vulnerabilities discovered remains unclear, raising concerns about the model's impact if released publicly.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights the risks of deploying powerful AI models without transparency on their effectiveness.

articles.title: The AI Slop Loop via @sejournal, @lilyraynyc
articles.snippet:
New examples show how AI search tools confidently cite fabricated SEO updates, exposing a growing risk in relying on LLM-generated information.
The post The AI Slop Loop appeared first on Search Engine Journal.
articles.ai_summary: AI search tools are increasingly misrepresenting information by confidently citing fabricated SEO updates, illustrating the dangers of relying on AI-generated content for accurate data.
articles.why_it_matters: Underscores the critical need for caution when using AI for information retrieval.
